title: Puerto Banus Dolphin Watching Full Day Boat Hire 2026 Guide
topic: dolphin watching full day boat hire in Puerto Banus
html:
Dolphin watching from a full-day boat hire in Puerto Banus offers a remarkable way to explore the Mediterranean alongside playful pods. Departing from Puerto Banus marina, you can expect shared tours from €90 per person or private motorboats from €350 for the day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified rentals to make this experience seamless.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Your dolphin-watching journey typically begins at Marina Puerto Banus, the glamorous harbour lined with superyachts and boutiques. Other real departure points include Cabopino Marina, Marbella Marina, and the small port at Estepona. Heading west, you might pass the Bay of Estepona or the waters off Gibraltar, where resident pods of common and striped dolphins are frequently sighted. A full-day trip often covers about 20 nautical miles, reaching prime dolphin territory near the Cabo de Gata coast or the Strait of Gibraltar. You can browse boats for your dates in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching

  1. Rigid Inflatable Boat (RIB), best for speed and agility, perfect for reaching dolphins quickly; accommodates 6, 8 guests; from €500 for a full day.
  2. Motorboat (e.g. Quicksilver Activ 605 Open), versatile for family groups, with enough deck space to spot dolphins; fits 8, 10 people; from €350 half-day. You can directly the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire for a specific model in Puerto Banus.
  3. Catamaran, stable and spacious for larger parties or relaxed cruises; up to 12 guests; from €800 full day.
  4. Luxury Yacht (e.g. Azimut or Sunseeker), offers comfort and style for a premium experience; 8, 12 guests; from €1,200 full day.
  5. Sailboat, a quiet, eco-friendly option for dolphin watching; 4, 6 guests; from €400 full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Expect to pay €90 to €2,500 per day depending on boat type and season. Shared dolphin-watching tours start at €90 per person. A small motorboat for half a day runs from €200. For a catamaran full-day charter, budget from €800. Crewed yacht charters with skipper and crew cost from €1,500 to €2,500. All prices are in EUR and typically exclude fuel and mooring fees. To compare exact rates, browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24 for real-time availability. One option worth a look is the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire.

  • Shared tour: from €90 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €200
  • Catamaran full-day: from €800
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from €1,500

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

For most motorboats under 15 metres length, a Spanish recreational licence is required for bareboat hire. However, many rentals include a skipper, meaning you can still enjoy the trip without a licence. The operator will confirm exact requirements based on the boat and your experience. For those looking to captain their own vessel, ensure you carry an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent. The high season runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind often creates calm seas ideal for dolphin watching. You can also see more at check availability.

For the best dolphin sightings, set off early in the morning when the sea is calm and dolphins are most active near the surface. The waters off Estepona and Gibraltar are known hotspots.

At a glance

  • Price band: €90, €2,500 per day (EUR)
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: Full day (6, 8 hours)
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests typical
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Puerto Banus?

Yes, you can hire a skipper from most operators, allowing you to enjoy the trip without a licence.

What is the best time of year for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

The summer months from June to September offer the most consistent sightings, with dolphins frequently spotted in the Strait of Gibraltar.

How far in advance should I book my boat?

For peak season, booking at least two weeks ahead is recommended to secure your preferred boat.

What should I bring on a dolphin-watching trip?

Bring sunscreen, a hat, water, and a camera; the boat usually provides life jackets and basic amenities.
